title: PM Modi congratulates Andy Burnham on becoming UK Prime Minister
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi congratulated Andy Burnham on his new role.  He expressed confidence that India-UK ties will deepen across key sectors.  The two nations share democratic values and cooperate widely in many areas.  With CETA entering into force, the partnership is poised for growth.  Modi looks forward to working closely to strengthen the comprehensive strategic partnership.

title: Argentina players could face FIFA bans after World Cup 2026 final chaos against Spain
content: FIFA has launched an investigation into post-match clashes following Argentina's World Cup final loss. Several Argentina players were involved in confrontations with Spanish counterparts after the game. FIFA's Disciplinary Committee has appointed a prosecutor to examine these potential breaches. Players found guilty of violent conduct face a minimum three-match suspension. The Argentine Football Association could also face disciplinary action and fines.

title: Who will ask if you’re home? The economy growing around urban loneliness
content: India faces a growing loneliness crisis as its population ages rapidly. Urbanization and changing social structures contribute to this increasing isolation. Young migrants and older adults alike experience profound feelings of not belonging. The emerging "silver economy" offers market-based solutions for connection and care. This situation highlights the need to address loneliness as a fundamental human requirement.

title: Engineers building Solar panels are using exact replicas of honeycombs made by Bees to ensure ...
content: Engineers have developed new solar panels inspired by bee honeycombs. This innovative design significantly improves sunlight absorption and power generation.  The honeycomb structure traps light, preventing reflection and increasing energy capture.  Tests show substantial power output gains compared to traditional flat panels.  This flexible design could allow solar technology on curved surfaces and vehicles.

title: 'Rohit aur Virat ko touch nahi kar payenge': Ashwin makes bold statement
content: Ravichandran Ashwin supports Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli's continued selection for India. He cited their extensive career achievements and significant fan support as key factors. Ashwin noted that bowlers do not receive the same level of public backing. Rohit Sharma recently silenced retirement speculation with a century against England. The captain stated that external criticism has always been part of his career.

title: US judge to Meta's laid off employees: Cannot stop the company from job cuts
content: A US judge has allowed Meta Platforms to proceed with planned layoffs of 26 employees. These employees had sued the company alleging AI-driven discrimination in job cuts. The judge declined an emergency request to halt the layoffs while claims proceed. He stated the court may reconsider if new evidence emerges about AI usage. Meta denies wrongdoing and states human decisions guided the workforce reduction.

title: 'The pain is immense': Lionel Messi pens emotional note after Argentina lose World Cup final
content: Spain defeated Argentina in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final after extra time. Lionel Messi shared an emotional message acknowledging the immense pain of the loss. He expressed pride in the team's achievement of reaching consecutive World Cup finals. Substitute Ferran Torres scored the decisive goal in the 106th minute of the match. The defeat ended Argentina's hopes of defending their title successfully.

title: Florida returned 500,000 oyster shells to Gulf, creating new reef habitat
content: Florida restored parts of the Gulf of Mexico by returning more than 500,000 tonnes of recycled oyster and clam shells to the sea between 2007 and 2024. The shells helped rebuild oyster reefs, improve water quality and support the return of fish, turtles and dolphins to levels not seen in over 25 years.

title: Aquarium fish escapes into crater lake, scientists fear biodiversity risk
content: Flowerhorn cichlids, bred by humans, have found their way into Lake Sampaloc, a crater lake in the Philippines. Their presence poses a significant threat to the local ecosystem, competing aggressively with native ayungin fish and disrupting aquaculture. Experts raise alarms about the potential for disease transmission and the rapid expansion of this invasive species, underscoring a pressing global issue of domesticated animals affecting natural habitats.

title: Airbnb CEO says will keep investing in New York despite Mamdani's rent freeze plan
content: Airbnb and Anthropic are expanding their New York City operations significantly.  This investment occurs despite concerns from business leaders about city policies.  Airbnb purchased a building for its growing New York workforce.  Anthropic will lease a large office building and double its staff.  These expansions highlight continued business activity amidst policy debates.

title: 118 cops & 60 marchers injured: Cops block protesters' march to Parl; Nadda meets CJP leaders
content: A protest by the Cockroach Janta Party demanding a minister's resignation turned chaotic. Protesters clashed with police, resulting in numerous injuries on both sides. Police reported detentions and filed charges related to rioting and assault on public servants. The BJP blamed Left-affiliated student groups for instigating the organized violence. Investigations are underway to establish criminal responsibility for the disturbances.

title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, cites trade disputes
content: President Trump imposed fifty percent tariffs on most Canadian goods. This action escalates trade tensions between the two nations. The tariffs apply to many goods previously protected under the USMCA agreement. This move heightens economic uncertainty and strains bilateral ties. Aides are examining the possibility of imposing additional tariffs on Canada.

title: Taylor Swift reportedly felt anxious about Blake lively drama before tying knot with Travis
content: Taylor Swift and Travis kelce are still getting criticised for not inviting Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds but it seems the popular pop star was anxious about the actress before getting marrried.

title: How Neymar Jr. turned childhood poverty into a journey to Football greatness
content: Neymar Jr is one of football’s biggest stars today, but his journey began in very difficult circumstances. A heartbreaking moment from his childhood became the reason he never stopped believing in himself. Years later, he turned that dream into reality. His story is a powerful reminder that even the toughest beginnings can lead to something extraordinary.

title: Ryan Clark was reportedly fired by ESPN amid Disney layoffs and internal tensions
content: Ryan Clark's decade-long run at ESPN ended abruptly after the network reportedly informed him of its decision during an episode of NFL Live. His exit comes as Disney implements widespread layoffs across ESPN and NFL Network. Reports suggest internal concerns over past on-air incidents and workplace tensions contributed to the decision. Despite the surprising departure, Clark's successful broadcasting career and popular podcast are expected to create new opportunities.

title: 'They will pay many times over': Trump as US carries out 10th night of Iran strikes
content: United States forces conducted tenth night of strikes against Iran.  These operations aim to reopen the Strait of Hormuz after recent attacks.  Iran also targeted US allies Kuwait, Jordan, and Bahrain with missile barrages.  Diplomatic efforts continue while the interim ceasefire agreement has collapsed.  Global energy supplies face fresh concerns as oil prices climb.

title: 'Ramayana' trailer reviews: Ranbir starrer hailed as 'SPECTACULAR'
content: Filmmakers and industry insiders have shared overwhelmingly positive early reactions to the 'Ramayana' trailer. They praised the film's grand scale, visual effects, and performances by the cast. Ranbir Kapoor as Lord Rama and Yash as Ravana received specific commendations for their roles. The trailer is set to be unveiled publicly on July 24. This cinematic adaptation of the Indian epic is slated for a Diwali 2026 worldwide release.

title: Vacant for decades? Why the government can't simply take over abandoned properties
content: In India, the absence of a comprehensive law governing abandoned buildings leads to fragmented regulations across various legislative acts. Municipal authorities hold the authority to inspect and classify buildings as dangerous. Owners are notified to undertake necessary repairs or secure their properties, with demolition a potential option in cases of imminent risk. Both Maharashtra and Delhi enforce mandatory structural evaluations for older buildings.

title: Messi suffers unwanted first in FIFA World Cup final as Spain silence Argentina icon
content: Spain defeated Argentina in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final after extra time. Lionel Messi experienced a rare off day, failing to create any chances for teammates. Spain's defensive strategy successfully contained the legendary Argentine forward throughout the match. Ferran Torres scored the decisive goal, securing Spain's second World Cup title. This victory extended Spain's impressive unbeaten international run to thirty-eight games.

title: Supreme Court orders new SIT into Ram temple donation theft, will monitor probe
content: The Supreme Court has taken over the Ayodhya Ram Mandir fund embezzlement investigation. Local police have been removed from the probe, which will now be led by a senior IPS officer. The Shri Ram Teerth Kshetra Trust must preserve all donation receipts and accounts for scrutiny. The court cautioned against politicizing the ongoing investigation into alleged fund pilferage. Eight individuals have already been arrested as the probe progresses impartially.

title: Day 1 of Parliament’s monsoon session washed out amid NEET, donation chori protests
content: The first day of Parliament's monsoon session was disrupted by opposition protests.  Opposition parties raised concerns over various issues, including temple donations and exam leaks.  Frequent adjournments led to the cancellation of proceedings in both houses.  A bill to increase the number of Supreme Court judges was introduced in Lok Sabha.  The changed seating arrangement for rebel TMC MPs also drew attention.

title: Andy Burnham pledges to end rough sleeping in Britain in his first speech as new UK PM
content: Andy Burnham has become the United Kingdom's seventh prime minister in ten years. He made his first pledge to end rough sleeping across the country. Burnham met the king at Buckingham Palace before heading to Downing Street. He promised to address the cost of living and build more council homes. The new prime minister aims to create a new political economic model.

title: 'The Odyssey' BO collection: Nolan's epic starring Matt Damon crosses Rs 80 crore mark in India
content: Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey crossed eighty crore rupees at the Indian box office. The epic fantasy adventure saw collections drop significantly on its first Monday. Still, the film continued its dominance, earning over eight crore rupees on day four. The English version remains the biggest contributor to its overall success. The Odyssey delivered Christopher Nolan's biggest opening day in India.

title: Defence ministry seeks firms for Rs 15,000 cr high-altitude airship project
content: India's defence ministry seeks domestic firms for airship-based pseudo satellites.  These platforms will provide persistent intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance capabilities.  The program aims to offer lower-cost alternatives to existing satellite systems.  Airships will hover in the stratosphere, bridging gaps between drones and satellites.  The Indian Air Force is actively partnering with local aerospace companies for this initiative.

title: ‘Worse than Jallianwala Bagh,’ says Bengal report on firing on Mamata-led rally in 1993
content: A commission report on the 1993 police firing was tabled in the assembly. The report stated the firing was unnecessary and worse than Jallianwala Bagh. Compensation of Rs 25 lakh was recommended for each bereaved family. The Chief Minister announced the government would provide this compensation. The commission also indicted several IPS officers for their role.

title: No decision on ethanol blending beyond 20%: Govt
content: The government has stated no decision exists to raise ethanol blending beyond twenty percent.  Lower-blended fuel or pure petrol will not be available at select outlets.  Any future diesel blending will depend on evaluations and stakeholder consultations.  The transition to E20 has been phased and evidence-based, with gradual increases.  Claims of vehicle damage from E20 are largely misleading or unsupported by technical evidence.

title: Youth full of aspirations, want country to move forward: PM Modi
content: Prime Minister Modi urged opposition parties to use logic and facts in Parliament.  He highlighted India's youth aspirations and recent national achievements.  The successful launch of a private space rocket showcased young Indian capabilities.  Recent inaugurations and a hydrogen train launch also marked progress.  India maintained growth despite global energy supply disruptions.

title: Supreme Court takes exception to meagre compensation for ex-HC judges
content: The Supreme Court has taken exception to low compensation for retired High Court judges.  It asked the Union government to form a committee for uniform guidelines.  This committee will consider basic minimum security and services for retired judges.  The panel will study issues faced by retired judges and submit recommendations.  The goal is to ensure retired judges lead a dignified post-retirement life.

title: NTA rebuts claims of 5, says NEET-UG OMR sheets forged
content: The National Testing Agency has refuted claims of OMR answer sheet discrepancies from five NEET-UG candidates.  Images circulated by these candidates were reportedly digitally altered or regenerated using artificial intelligence.  The agency verified the original OMR sheets and scores for all five students involved.  These actions follow allegations of swapped OMR sheets and fewer attempted answers than recorded.  Candidates submitting fabricated records may face severe penalties under relevant acts.

title: Rahul Gandhi claims PM Modi ‘most anti-youth PM India has seen’
content: Opposition leaders criticized Prime Minister Narendra Modi for his government's actions.  They condemned the crackdown on young protesters marching towards Parliament.  Rahul Gandhi called Modi the most anti-youth Prime Minister India has seen.  Leaders stated that students are not criminals and their demands are legitimate.  The protests highlight widespread discontent regarding examination irregularities and education system issues.

title: Rain continues to play havoc in Jammu, toll goes up to 28
content: Torrential rain caused flash floods and landslides, killing twenty-eight people across Jammu division.  Poonch reported twenty-three deaths, including several children, after a house collapse.  Rescuers dug four bodies from rubble left by raging streams and collapsing hillsides.  Amarnath Yatra and pilgrimages to Vaishno Devi shrine remained suspended for a second day.  The India Meteorological Department issued a red alert warning of extremely heavy rain.

title: Maria Goretti says she misses financial independence after quitting MTV career for motherhood
content: Former MTV VJ Maria Goretti has opened up about stepping away from a successful television career after marrying actor Arshad Warsi, admitting that while she sometimes misses earning her own money and the appreciation that came with her work, she has no regrets about choosing motherhood. Maria also spoke about witnessing the ups and downs of husband Arshad Warsi's Bollywood journey, saying those experiences helped keep them grounded.

title: CJP protest in Delhi LIVE: Protesters reclaim Jantar Mantar protest site; Wangchuk to continue indefinite fast
content: The police action on one of the largest protests in the national capital in recent years left several people injured

title: The fine art of creating blood donors at home; and resources for those in Chennai seeking to donate blood
content: A leitmotif among blood donation organisations is the increasing difficulty in finding people with “generous veins”; and the solution could strangely be found in the living rooms of households, as illustrated by these two accounts from Chennai of parents inspiring their children to join the cause

title: TVK government in Tamil Nadu faces a fiscal test in balancing populism and prudence
content: “We can have a welfare State only if we work for it and step up production,” said the then Chief Minister C. Rajagopalachari in June 1952

title: Sonam Wangchuk to continue fast, cites police action against protesters
content: Mt. Wangchuk said he is "touched and moved" by the manner in which the protesters have maintained peace despite "provocation"

title: Panchayat, local body elections in Rajasthan to be completed by November 15
content: The Bharatiya Janata Party government submitted the schedule for panchayat and local body elections in the Rajasthan High Court during the hearing on a batch of public interest petitions

title: Gujarat destroys drugs worth ₹2,153 crore at Dahej
content: “The Gujarat Police have dealt a major blow to international drug smuggling networks through decisive action against drug mafias and cartels,” Mr. Sanghavi

title: Six killed in floods, landslips in Northeast
content: Railway authorities cancelled, diverted, or short-terminated several passenger train services in Assam. At least 25 people have been killed across four States since the last week of June

title: Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary flags off special train carrying 1,100 pilgrims to Somnath Dham
content: Bihar government will bear the entire cost of the seven-day pilgrimage. Free pilgrimage will take place from July 20 to 26

title: Assam unveils mission to convert Muga silk waste into high-value yarn
content: Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma says that about 40% of the cocoons yielding the golden silk currently go unused due to the absence of proper processing facilities

title: ED provisionally attached 389 immovable properties worth ₹240.03 Crore
content: ED initiated investigation on the basis of multiple FIRs registered by the Uttar Pradesh Police and the Delhi Police against BIIPL, GVCTPL, Satinder Singh Bhasin and others, under various Sections of the Indian Penal Code, 1860

title: BMC BJP corporator demands regular audit of the birth registration
content: Several corporators highlighted that the delay in birthday certificates is derailing study-abroad plans, visa applications for scores of residents, and document problems

title: More than half of Odisha’s PVTG adult women anaemic, says a study by Utkal University
content: Among PVTGs, the Hill Kharia shows the highest stunting prevalence at 82.76%, a distressing figure that signifies a critical public health emergency, the study raises alarm

title: Aston Villa signs João Gomes as star Morgan Rogers completes British record move to Chelsea
content: Gomes arrives days after club record signing Johan Manzambi, who cost more than 50 million pounds ($67 million) from Freiburg after a stellar World Cup with Switzerland

title: Supreme Court proposes SIT probe into Ram Temple case
content: Bench tells petitioners not to play politics with the donation embezzlement case; complainants had sought a CBI probe and publication of all financial details of Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust; court lists case for orders on July 27

title: 7 killed in landslide in Jammu and Kashmir as rain toll hits 21
content: The J&K Police actively participated in the rescue operation at Domail Dhok after a tragic landslide hit a mud house, claiming seven lives; rescue efforts are continuing in coordination with all agencies concerned, said the police

title: Monsoon Session off to a stormy start amid Opposition protests
content: Opposition MPs raise NEET paper leak, Ram Temple donation theft issues; Lok Sabha adjourned after repeated disruptions; MPs of Congress, SP and Trinamool troop into the Well of the House; DMK raises Mekedatu issue, skips Congress-led meeting

title: Several hurt in crackdown on CJP Parliament march
content: Scores hospitalised with fractures, head and eye injuries; CJP leaders meet Nadda, demand Pradhan’s ouster; Wangchuk cites ‘brutality’, says will continue fast till MPs meet ‘youth leaders’ or him

title: Police outnumbered, taken aback by number of protesters who reached Parliament’s doorstep
content: In an unprecedented turn of events, despite multiple layers of barricades, deployment of around 3,000 security personnel and an Internet shutdown, large crowds comprising mostly students and young people were able to reach the doorsteps of Parliament before they were tear-gassed and charged with batons

title: Structural stability assessment of KWA reservoirs in Kochi begins
content: The French firm SUEZ has begun non-destructive testing of the water utility’s reservoirs, pump houses and other infrastructure

title: Former CorroHealth staff say compensation promise is a relief
content: Government-mediated talks resulted in the company agreeing to pay five months’ gross salary as additional compensation for laid-off employees, says Minister; compensation to be paid on July 26

title: Defections affect moral sanctity of democracy
content: The greatest victim of political defections is not the Opposition party that loses a legislator. It is the voter

title: Political rivalries clash over Karnataka’s Bidadi township project
content: The Bidadi township project envisages acquiring about 9,600 acres across 25 villages in the Bengaluru South district.

title: Indus Waters Treaty will not remain operational in its present form, says official
content: He says India has placed the treaty in “abeyance” after the Pahalgam terror attack and ever since there has been no change in Pakistan’s sponsorship of terrorists

title: Extra posts sanctioned in 53 govt., aided schools in Kerala when student strength rose by one each
content: Posts sanctioned from primary to high school classes in the 2023-24, 2024-25, and 2025-26 academic years

title: No new diarrhoea cases reported in Giripuram, officials confirm
content: Cautioning against misinformation circulating on social media platforms, District Medical and Health Officer reiterated that the reports are fake and that the situation is under control

title: Nithin Raj murder: M.K. Ram arrested, court orders his release
content: Court finds procedural lapses in arrest. The former teacher of Kannur Dental College had been absconding for weeks after the High Court rejected his anticipatory bail in connection with the death of BDS student Nithin Raj. He was arrested in Coorg on Monday. Nithin’s family blames caste-based harassment for his suicide

title: NLC India Renewables Limited appoints book running lead managers for IPO
content: The BRLMs shall advise the company in the preparation and management of the IPO, including undertaking due diligence, preparation of offer documents and coordination with regulatory authorities and intermediaries, NLCIL says

title: Special court in Mumbai sentences 44 Somalian pirates to life imprisonment; praises Navy for prompt action
content: In March 2024, a hijacking alert was received from Iranian flagged FV Al Kambar, leading to the arrest of nine Somalian nationals after INS Trushul and Sumedha responded

title: Kidnapping accused killed in police encounter in Gujarat’s Anand
content: The accused was shot in self-defence after he allegedly attacked two policemen with a concealed knife while attempting to escape from custody, police said

title: Sexual assault-accused trainee IPS officer found unconscious
content: Meanwhile, Hyderabad police said they had no information about the hospital incident and that no separate case had been registered in connection with it.

title: Karnataka HC dismisses PIL complaining procedural lapse in one notification of acquiring 518 acres for Bidadi township project
content: However, court clarifies that this order is not an expression of opinion on other issues related to acquisition notifications

title: Auto Drivers’ Association protests against illegal autorickshaw operations in Kalaburagi
content: Members of the Kalyana Karnataka Auto Drivers Association staged a protest in Kalaburagi on Monday, alleging a rise in the operation of unauthorised autorickshaws and app-based transport services that they claim are functioning in violation of transport regulations.

title: Corruption allegations without credible evidence can ‘tarnish’ reputation of judicial officers, SC says
content: The top court was hearing activist Gulshan Pahuja’s plea challenging a High Court order refusing to extend his time to surrender; he has been sentenced to six months’ imprisonment for criminal contempt over allegedly derogatory videos targeting judicial officers

title: Kalaburagi Police launch ‘Beda Bro’ and ‘Operation RISE’ anti-drug awareness campaigns
content: The Kalaburagi City and District Police on Monday launched the “No Bro” and “Operation RISE” anti-drug awareness campaign to strengthen efforts against drug abuse, trafficking and illegal sale of narcotic substances across the district.

title: Nine arrested in Guntur assault case
content: Superintendent of Police warns that strict legal action will be taken against anyone spreading false information, making objectionable remarks about the woman or circulating content that harms her dignity through social media or other platforms

title: Fishers allege Mamallan reservoir plan ignores tidal inundation risks
content: They say that the project had been designed without accounting for tidal movements and storm surges from Bay of Bengal, and argued that the technical assessment by IIT-M had not adequately assessed this risk. They fear the project would eliminate about 4,375 acres of natural flood storage

title: Is all well in Balen Shah's Nepal? Why protests are testing his promise of change
content: A digital news desk journalist at The Times of India with over two years of experience. Closely tracks foreign affairs and international politics. Fondness for precise language and clever headlines. When not editing news, he is usually refining a turn of phrase. Read More

title: CJP Parliament March LIVE Updates: "Protests To Continue," Says CJP After Meeting With JP Nadda
content: CJP Parliament March LIVE Updates: Union minister JP Nadda and CJP members Saurav Das and Ashutosh Ranka met in Delhi on Monday.

title: Son Of Brazil's Ex-President Bolsonaro Secures US Green Card
content: Eduardo Bolsonaro said he moved to protect himself from what he called unjust persecution by Brazil's Supreme Court, not help his father.

title: 7 Killed, Several Trapped In Sikkim Tunnel After Landslide Triggers Gas Leak
content: Officials said methane levels inside the tunnel remained high initially that rescuers were unable to reach the location

title: "Discriminatory Treatment": US Imposes New 50% Tariffs On Certain Canadian Goods
content: US President Donald Trump signed orders Monday to impose new 50-percent tariffs on some Canadian goods, citing "discriminatory treatment" in the areas of alcohol, automobiles and dairy products.

title: France Says Two Of Its Diplomats "Detained, Physically Abused" In Iran
content: Two French diplomats in Iran suffered "an extremely serious act of intimidation" by Islamic republic security services, with one of them physically abused, France's foreign minister said Monday.

title: Monsoon Car Damage? Here's When Insurance Pays And When It Won't
content: Instead of attempting repairs or restarting the engine, owners should immediately inform their insurer, click photographs or record videos of damage.

title: On Camera, 3 Men In Bulletproof Vests Loot Jewellery Shop In Maharashtra
content: To discourage witnesses from intervening, they fired shots in the air before speeding away on their motorcycle.

title: Jordan Says It Downed 3 Iranian Missiles Targeting Kingdom
content: Jordan's army said on Monday that air defences had downed three Iranian missiles targeting the kingdom, with no injuries or damage reported.

title: Trump Discusses Trade, Military Alliance, Hormuz With New UK PM
content: US President Donald Trump said on Monday he had a "very good conversation" with new British Prime Minister Andy Burnham, adding he would be meeting him "in the not too distant future."

title: 118 Cops Injured, 70 Detained After CJP Protest Turns Violent In Delhi
content: CJP Protest: Police said around 70 protesters have been detained and legal proceedings have been initiated.

title: US Slams "Dangerous" China Action In Sea Clash With Philippines Navy
content: Washington on Monday condemned what it called "dangerous and aggressive" action by China, whose personnel are accused of injuring a Filipino sailor at a shoal in the disputed South China Sea.

title: 27 Dead, 83 Missing From Capsised Guyana Ferry
content: At least 27 people are dead and 83 more are missing after a ferry capsized off the coast of Guyana over the weekend, Prime Minister Mark Phillips said Monday.

title: Missiles Hit US Base In Kuwait As Iran Declares "Full-Scale War"
content: The Iranian army said early Tuesday it had targeted a US base in Kuwait with missiles, as President Masoud Pezeshkian said the conflict had morphed into a "full-scale war".

title: Wes Streeting Named New UK Defence Minister
content: Britain's new Prime Minister Andy Burnham named ex-health minister Wes Streeting as his defence secretary late Monday, the government said, as ministerial appointments ran into the night.

title: US Launches New Round Of Strikes On Iran, Now 10th Night In A Row
content: The United States military said late Monday it was carrying out its 10th consecutive night of strikes on Iran in a push to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Class 11 Student Stabbed To Death In Delhi, 2 Accused Arrested: Cops
content: A 19-year-old Class 11 student was allegedly stabbed to death following a quarrel in Delhi's Kalkaji area on Monday, with police apprehending two accused in connection with the murder.

title: Israel's Leading Leftist Party Launches Electoral Campaign
content: Israel's largest leftist party on Monday launched its campaign for the legislative election due to be held in October, after having selected its list of candidates in the primaries.

title: 20 Killed, 100 Missing In Afghanistan Flooding
content: Only mud remained where riverside shops previously stood in Parun, the provincial capital where residents were searching for their mayor among others.

title: Netanyahu Won't Be Arrested In Any Shape Or Form: Trump After Mamdani Threat
content: "Benjamin Netanyahu will not be arrested, in any way, shape, or form, while in the United States of America," Trump wrote.

title: Trainee IPS Officer Accused Of Sexual Harassment Attempts Suicide
content: The complainant accused him of recording a private video of her without her knowledge or consent and sending it to her husband in an attempt to blackmail her.

title: Supreme Court seeks more exclusive UAPA, NDPS courts to speed up trials
content: additional solicitor general Aishwarya Bhati informed a bench of CJI Surya Kant and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and V Mohana that 14 additional exclusive NIA special courts have been designated in nine states . the courts include three each in Gujarat and Jharkhand, two in Maharashtra and one each in J&K, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Manipur, Assam and Punjab .

title: More headache for int'l students: US to roll out fixed-period F-1 visa from Sept
content: Lubna Kably is a senior editor, who focuses on various policies and legislation. In particular, she writes extensively on immigration and tax policies. The Indian diaspora is the largest in the world; through her articles she demystifies the immigration-policy related developments in select countries for outbound students, job aspirants and employees. She also analyses the impact of Income-tax and GST related developments for individuals and business entities. Read More

title: Can't force parents to create Apaar IDs for students: Supreme Court
content: the order would apply to 24 crore students . parents cannot be mandated to create 'one student, one unique ID' for their children . schools are mandating parents to provide 'aadhaar number' of their children, expert says .

content: 80,000-plus spectators greeted him with lusty boos as he strode onto the field for the trophy presentation . supporters generated enough cheers to produce enough cheer to produce the trophy . a u.s. president has remained planted centre stage like an enthusiastic wedding guest .

title: Four Indians killed in Russian strike on vessel off Ukrainian port
content: four Indian nationals were reported to have died in an attack on a commercial vessel . at the time of the incident, there were 17 crew members on board, including 5 Indians . india condemned the attack, reaffirming that targeting commercial shipping is deplorable and must be avoided .

title: US identifies two soldiers killed in Jordan, launches attack on Iran for 10th night
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. title: Indus Water Treaty in present form no longer exists, Pak aiding terrorism: India
content: the indus waters treaty with Pakistan will no longer function in its present form . a source said it's not in India's interest to remain a party to the treaty anymore . the government has concluded hardening its position on the contentious water issue .
